source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
We want to paint each square in the grid with the colours P, Q, R and S, so that neighbouring squares always have different colours. (Squares which share the same corner point also count as neighbouring.) Some of the squares are already painted. In which colour(s) could the grey square be painted?
<image1>

image_dependency: question refers to a specific grid configuration, pre-painted squares, and a grey target square whose states are not fully specified in text alone
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows an available image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: Solving requires checking coloring constraints on neighboring squares, including diagonal adjacency ('share the same corner point')
The task asks for all valid colors for one target square, which requires combining multiple surrounding constraints rather than reading a single fact
choices has 5 options including a multi-answer possibility ('either R or S'), indicating constraint evaluation over several candidate colors
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and answer_text is a concise categorical result ('either R or S')
Multiple-choice options are provided, so the final answer can be matched against a fixed option set
A checker could verify the result by inspecting the image-defined neighbors and eliminating invalid colors

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, so the core semantic assets are complete
asset registry passed with no recorded issues or risk flags
high image dependency with the required image available makes this a strong image-grounded sample
multi-step score and verifiability score are both high, indicating good collection value and checkability
